description: Canonical broad-category Wikipedia retrieval development set. This is a dev harness, not a holdout benchmark: it mixes BM25 wins, BM25 regressions, and hard procedural questions to test whether Wikipedia access improves Gemma answers without increasing catastrophic failures.

questions.reason: BM25 huge rescue over direct; caustic sodium silicate/waterglass procedure with safety-critical dilution.
BM25 huge rescue over direct; charcoal kiln vs biogas vs waterwheel comparison exposes wood-use misconception.
BM25 huge rescue over direct; unknown-liquid identification tests whether retrieval prevents unsafe flame/sniffing advice.
BM25 catastrophic regression; pulley ratio directionality and belt-drive safety are easy to invert.
BM25 catastrophic regression; white-phosphorus handling tests whether retrieval prevents dry brushing/oil mistakes.
BM25 regression; rope splice directionality tests whether the agent verifies against-the-lay vs with-the-lay.
Non-fail BM25 lift; ecology and food-security critique tests whether retrieval adds concrete details.
BM25 rescue but still weak; high-stakes breech-birth maneuver tests exact procedural source use.
Moderate BM25 loss; pendulum/water-clock constants test whether retrieval preserves numeric accuracy.
Moderate BM25 loss; isolation-zone workflow tests whether retrieval adds details without confusing flow direction.

target.notes: Lift should be interpreted with auto-fail and zero-rate side by side; do not hide catastrophic failures behind median-only reporting.
